claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 1 child:
  - Alex is 17 years old and lives with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 17 and in approved education
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

expected_eligibility.name: Alex
expected_eligibility.reason: Claimant lives in the UK; Child is 17 and in approved education; Child lives with claimant; Child is not in local authority care; Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation; Child is not fostered; Child does not work 24+ hours/week; Child has not started an apprenticeship in England; Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right
circumstances: Child is 17 and in approved education
Child is not in local authority care
Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Hello! I'd be happy to help you determine your eligibility for Child Benefit. 

To get started, I need to gather some information about you and your children. Let me begin with a simple question:

**How many children do you have that you'd like to claim Child Benefit for?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Hello! I'm interested in claiming Child Benefit. I have one child I'd like to claim for.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information! So you have one child you'd like to claim Child Benefit for.

**What is your child's name?**

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you! Now I have a few questions to assess your eligibility for Child Benefit for Alex.

**How old is Alex?**

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you. Now, let me ask about your living situation:

**Does Alex live with you?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, Alex lives at home with me.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Good, thank you. Now I need to understand your residency status:

**Are you a UK resident, and do you have the right to reside in the UK?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I live in the UK - I'm in Manchester.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for confirming that. I need to gather a bit more detail about your residency status to properly assess eligibility:

**What is your immigration status in the UK? For example, are you a British citizen, or do you have settled status, a visa, or another type of immigration permission?**

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Excellent, thank you for that information. Now I have one more important question:

**Is Alex in full-time education or training?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, Alex is in sixth form college studying for A-levels.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Thank you for providing all that information. I now have enough details to assess your eligibility. Let me review the rules to make sure I give you an accurate decision.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Based on the information you've provided and the eligibility rules I've reviewed, I can now provide you with my assessment:

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
**Great news! You are eligible to claim Child Benefit for Alex.**

Here's why:

- **Alex's age**: At 17, Alex is under 20 years old
- **Approved education**: Alex is in full-time sixth form college studying for A-levels, which counts as approved education
- **Living arrangements**: Alex lives with you
- **Your status**: You're a British citizen and UK resident, responsible for Alex

According to the Child Benefit eligibility rules, you normally qualify if your child is under 20 and stays in approved education or training. Alex meets this requirement, so your claim should be successful.

If you have any questions or if Alex's circumstances change (such as leaving full-time education), please contact HMRC's Child Benefit Office for further guidance.

reasonings: Alex is eligible for Child Benefit. The key eligibility criteria are met: (1) Alex is 17 years old, which is under 20; (2) Alex is in approved education (sixth form college studying for A-levels), which qualifies as approved education or training; (3) Alex lives with you; (4) You are a British citizen and UK resident; (5) You are responsible for Alex. According to the rules, you normally still qualify for Child Benefit if your child is under 20 and they stay in approved education or training.
arguments.overall_reasoning: As a British citizen and UK resident responsible for 17-year-old Alex who lives with you and is in full-time approved education (A-levels at sixth form), you meet all the eligibility requirements for Child Benefit. Your claim for Alex should be successful."}

According to the rules, you normally still qualify for Child Benefit if your child is under 20 and they stay in approved education or training.

response.overall_reasoning: As a British citizen and UK resident responsible for 17-year-old Alex who lives with you and is in full-time approved education (A-levels at sixth form), you meet all the eligibility requirements for Child Benefit. Your claim for Alex should be successful."}
